residence charon

residence charon

Our Vision

Retrofit Construction's inaugural milestone emerged through a deep energy retrofit undertaken on a 130-year-old duplex nestled in Montreal's Point Saint-Charles district. Founder Fellipe Falluh orchestrated a masterful integration of three distinct objectives: a primary family residence, an income-generating property, and a pioneering business pilot. At its core, the initiative embraced a comprehensive envelope retrofit with an unwavering emphasis on achieving impeccable airtightness. This translated into a transition to triple-glazed windows, augmented insulation enveloping both the interior and exterior, and the integration of a resilient, permanent HVAC system for both dwelling units. The project extended beyond the structural, encompassing contemporary interior enhancements, crowned by a three-story extension along the southern facade, culminating in a captivating rooftop terrace. This impressive endeavor garnered widespread attention, featuring prominently in various media outlets and even gracing the cover of a prestigious magazine. A noteworthy trajectory awaits, as the property positions itself to claim the distinction of Quebec's first net-zero ready certified establishment.

The project that started it all for retrofit construction was the deep energy retrofit of a 130 year old duplex in the borough of Point Saint-Charles in montreal. When the time came to look for property to move into, Fellipe Falluh, the founder of Retrofit Construction combined three projects into one: main residence for his family, a revenue property and a pilot project for his business.

The project consisted of a full envelope retrofit in which air tightness was at the forefront of it all. All the windows were changed to triple glazing, insulation was added to the interior and exterior and a high permanent HVAC system was added to both units. All the interiors were updated and a three floor extension was added on the south facade giving access to a rooftop terrace. It was featured in several press posts and on the cover of a magazine. It is currently on its way to become the first net-zero ready certified property in quebec.

Completed in 2023 | Construction costs: 500k

[charon residence]

retrofitting and renovating of a duplex in psc (Qc), Canada, 2023.
